key_insight: A weighted-graph abstraction turns the complicated mesh-overlap communication pattern in B-spline s-version FEM into a tractable domain-decomposition problem for distributed-memory parallelization.
engineering_relevance: This makes locally refined BSFEM/SFEM practical for large-scale solid mechanics and multiphysics simulations on clusters by simplifying communication design and balancing workload.
method: Model overlapping mesh interactions as a weighted graph.
Use graph partitioning to build distributed-memory domain decomposition and communication structures.
Assign node weights to better balance localized computational loads.
evidence_signal: The approach was validated on a 3D steady Poisson problem, demonstrating distributed-memory parallel computation and reduced total runtime with weighted load balancing.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the graph model and partitioning strategy, so irregular overlap patterns may still create communication overhead or load imbalance.
paper_title: Distributed-memory parallelization of B-spline based s-version of the finite element method via weighted graph abstraction
